title: tennantia sennii
intro: Tennantia sennii, also known as Senna, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to tropical Africa. The medicinal properties of Tennantia sennii lie in its anthraquinone glycosides, which have been used to treat various gastrointestinal issues, including constipation and irritable bowel syndrome. The health benefits of Tennantia sennii include its ability to stimulate bowel movements, relieve symptoms of diverticulitis, and treat hemorrhoids. Tennantia sennii can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsules, or extracts, and is often used in traditional medicine, although it should be consumed under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential side effects.
